packages.name: sharp
packages.synopsis: Package sharp is a high-level, fluent image-processing library for Go, built entirely on the standard library (image, image/color, image/png, image/jpeg, image/gif; BMP and uncompressed TIFF codecs are implemented in-package).
packages.doc:
Package sharp is a high-level, fluent image-processing library for Go, built
entirely on the standard library (image, image/color, image/png, image/jpeg,
image/gif; BMP and uncompressed TIFF codecs are implemented in-package). It
is inspired by the ergonomics of the Node.js "sharp" library: you build a
pipeline, chain operations, and export to a format.

# The pipeline

A Pipeline wraps an in-memory RGBA image. Construct one from a decoded image,
a file, a byte slice or an io.Reader:

	p := sharp.New(img)                 // from an image.Image
	p := sharp.FromFile("in.png")       // decode a file
	p := sharp.FromBytes(data)          // decode PNG/JPEG bytes
	p := sharp.FromReader(r)            // decode from a reader

Operations are methods that return the same *Pipeline, so they chain. They
are applied eagerly and mutate the pipeline's internal buffer (the source
image passed to New is copied first and never modified). If any step fails,
the error is stored and every later operation is skipped; the error surfaces
from the terminal export call or from Err. All operations are deterministic:
identical input always yields identical output.

	out, err := sharp.FromFile("in.jpg").
		Resize(sharp.ResizeOptions{Width: 800, Fit: sharp.FitContain}).
		Grayscale().
		Sharpen(1).
		ToJPEG(85)

# Operations

Geometry:
  - Resize with Nearest, Bilinear, Cubic (Catmull-Rom), Mitchell or Lanczos3
    sampling and FitExact/FitContain/FitCover.
  - Affine (arbitrary 2x3 matrix, bilinear) and Trim (auto-crop borders).
  - Crop / Extract a rectangular region, Extend (pad) with a fill colour.
  - Rotate90/180/270 (exact) and Rotate (arbitrary angle, bilinear).
  - FlipVertical/Flip and FlipHorizontal/Flop.

Colour and tone:
  - Grayscale, Negate/Invert, Tint, Brightness, Contrast, Gamma,
    Saturation, Threshold.
  - Normalise (contrast stretch), Linear (a*x+b per channel),
    Modulate (brightness/saturation/hue/lightness) and CLAHE.

Convolution and morphology:
  - Blur (separable Gaussian), Sharpen, Unsharp (sigma-based unsharp mask)
    and a generic Convolve(Kernel).
  - Median, Erode, Dilate and a generic Morphology (open/close).

Channels and bands:
  - ExtractChannel, JoinChannels, RemoveAlpha, EnsureAlpha, Recomb (colour
    matrix) and bitwise Boolean/Bandbool operations.

Composition:
  - Composite another image with alpha blending, gravity/offset placement and
    a choice of blend modes (multiply, screen, overlay, darken, lighten, ...).
  - Flatten onto a solid background colour, removing transparency.

# Metadata and statistics

Metadata reports the current width, height, detected source format, channel
count, alpha presence, density and colour space. Stats computes per-channel
means. Clone returns an independent copy of a pipeline so a partially-built
pipeline can be branched.

# Codecs

Input decodes PNG, JPEG, GIF, BMP and uncompressed TIFF (the last two
implemented in-package), plus raw pixel buffers via FromRaw. Output supports
PNG, JPEG, GIF, BMP, TIFF and raw bytes. There is no standard-library codec
for WebP or AVIF, so those formats are out of scope.

# Output

Terminal methods materialise the result:

	img, err := p.ToImage()      // the image.Image
	buf, err := p.ToPNG()        // PNG bytes
	buf, err := p.ToJPEG(90)     // JPEG bytes at quality 90
	buf, err := p.ToGIF()        // GIF bytes
	buf, err := p.ToBMP()        // BMP bytes
	buf, err := p.ToTIFF()       // TIFF bytes
	buf, err := p.ToRaw()        // raw RGBA bytes
	err := p.ToFile("out.png", sharp.FormatPNG, 0)

# Determinism and colour handling

Images are held as non-premultiplied 8-bit RGBA. Channel arithmetic is done
in floating point and clamped to [0,255]. JPEG output has no alpha, so the
encoder flattens transparency onto black; call Flatten first to control the
background.

vars.doc: Black, White and Transparent are common fill colours.

types.name: AffineOptions
types.doc: AffineOptions configures an Affine transform.

types.name: BlendMode
types.signature: type BlendMode int
types.doc:
BlendMode selects how overlay (source) colours are combined with base
(backdrop) colours during Composite. The default, BlendOver, is ordinary
source-over alpha compositing; the others are the separable blend modes from
the W3C compositing specification, applied per channel in [0,1] before the
alpha composite.

consts.signature:
const (
	BlendOver BlendMode = iota
	BlendMultiply
	BlendScreen
	BlendOverlay
	BlendDarken
	BlendLighten
	BlendColorDodge
	BlendColorBurn
	BlendHardLight
	BlendSoftLight
	BlendDifference
	BlendExclusion
	BlendAdd
)

types.name: BoolOp
types.signature: type BoolOp int
types.doc: BoolOp selects a bitwise operation for Boolean and Bandbool.

types.name: CLAHEOptions
types.doc: CLAHEOptions configures Contrast Limited Adaptive Histogram Equalisation.

types.name: Channel
types.signature: type Channel int
types.doc: Channel identifies one band of an RGBA image.

types.name: ChannelStat
types.signature:
type ChannelStat struct {
	Min    uint8
	Max    uint8
	Mean   float64
	StdDev float64
	Sum    float64
}
types.doc:
ChannelStat holds summary statistics for a single colour band across all
pixels of an image. Min and Max are the extreme sample values (0-255); Mean
and StdDev are the arithmetic mean and population standard deviation of the
samples; Sum is the total of all samples in the band.

types.name: CompositeOptions
types.doc: CompositeOptions configures how an overlay image is combined with the base.

types.name: ExtendMode
types.signature: type ExtendMode int
types.doc: ExtendMode selects how the border pixels added by ExtendWith are filled.

consts.doc:
Extend fill strategies. They mirror the extendWith options of the Node sharp
library.

types.name: ExtendOptions
types.signature:
type ExtendOptions struct {
	Top        int
	Right      int
	Bottom     int
	Left       int
	Background RGBA
	Mode       ExtendMode
}
types.doc:
ExtendOptions configures an ExtendWith operation. Top, Right, Bottom and Left
give the padding in pixels on each side (negative values are treated as
zero). Background is used only when Mode is ExtendBackground.

types.name: Fit
types.signature: type Fit int
types.doc:
Fit controls how the target dimensions are interpreted when both a width and
a height are supplied to Resize.

types.name: Format
types.signature: type Format string
types.doc: Format identifies an encoded image format.

consts.signature:
const (
	FormatUnknown Format = ""
	FormatPNG     Format = "png"
	FormatJPEG    Format = "jpeg"
	FormatGIF     Format = "gif"
	FormatBMP     Format = "bmp"
	FormatTIFF    Format = "tiff"
	FormatRaw     Format = "raw"
)

types.name: Gravity
types.signature: type Gravity int
types.doc: Gravity anchors an overlay or region relative to the base image.

consts.signature:
const (
	GravityTopLeft Gravity = iota
	GravityTop
	GravityTopRight
	GravityLeft
	GravityCenter
	GravityRight
	GravityBottomLeft
	GravityBottom
	GravityBottomRight
)

types.name: Histogram
types.doc:
Histogram holds 256-bin intensity histograms for an image. R, G and B count
the occurrences of each 8-bit value in the respective colour band, and
Luminance counts the rounded Rec. 601 luma of every pixel.

types.name: Interpolation
types.signature: type Interpolation int
types.doc: Interpolation selects the sampling algorithm used when resizing or rotating.

consts.doc:
Additional high-quality, separable resampling kernels selectable through
ResizeOptions.Interpolation. They continue the Interpolation enumeration
started in resize.go (Nearest = 0, Bilinear = 1).

types.name: JPEGOptions
types.doc: JPEGOptions configures JPEG encoding.

types.name: Kernel
types.doc:
Kernel is a square convolution kernel. Weights are stored row-major and Size
must be odd (1, 3, 5, ...). Divisor and Offset are applied after the weighted
sum: out = sum/Divisor + Offset. A zero Divisor is treated as 1.

funcs.name: NewKernel
funcs.signature: func NewKernel(weights []float64, divisor, offset float64) (Kernel, error)
funcs.doc:
NewKernel builds a Kernel from a square slice of weights, inferring the size.
It returns an error if len(weights) is not a positive odd perfect square.

types.name: Metadata
types.doc: Metadata describes the current image held by a pipeline.

types.name: ModulateOptions
types.doc:
ModulateOptions configures a Modulate operation. Multipliers default to 1 and
additive/rotational terms default to 0 when left as the zero value.

types.name: MorphOp
types.signature: type MorphOp int
types.doc: MorphOp selects a morphological operation for Morphology.

types.name: NormaliseOptions
types.doc: NormaliseOptions configures a Normalise (contrast-stretch) operation.

types.name: PNGOptions
types.doc: PNGOptions configures PNG encoding.

types.name: Pipeline
types.doc:
Pipeline is a fluent, chainable image-processing pipeline. Construct one with
New, FromFile or FromBytes, chain any number of operations, then materialise
the result with ToImage, ToPNG, ToJPEG or ToFile.

Operations are applied eagerly and mutate the pipeline's internal RGBA
buffer. If any step fails, the error is retained and every subsequent
operation becomes a no-op; the error surfaces from the terminal call or from
Err. All operations are deterministic.

funcs.name: FromBytes
funcs.doc:
FromBytes decodes an encoded image (PNG or JPEG) from a byte slice and
returns a pipeline seeded with it. The detected format is recorded and
reported by Metadata.

funcs.name: FromFile
funcs.doc: FromFile reads and decodes an image file from disk.

funcs.name: FromRaw
funcs.signature: func FromRaw(pix []byte, width, height, channels int) *Pipeline
funcs.doc:
FromRaw builds a pipeline from raw pixel bytes. channels must be 1
(grayscale), 3 (RGB) or 4 (RGBA); the data length must equal
width*height*channels. Grayscale expands to equal RGB with opaque alpha and
RGB gets opaque alpha.

funcs.name: FromReader
funcs.doc:
FromReader decodes an encoded image from r. The reader is fully consumed so
the in-package BMP and TIFF decoders can inspect the magic number.

funcs.name: JoinChannels
funcs.doc:
JoinChannels builds a new pipeline by combining single-band images into the
R, G, B and (optionally) A channels of one image. Each source contributes its
red channel as the band value. Exactly 3 or 4 images must be supplied and all
must share the same dimensions; with 3 the result is fully opaque.

funcs.name: New
funcs.doc:
New creates a pipeline from an already-decoded image. The source pixels are
copied into an internal RGBA buffer, so the caller's image is never mutated.

methods.name: Affine
methods.doc:
Affine applies an arbitrary 2x3 affine transform to the image, sampling the
source bilinearly. The matrix m = [a, b, c, d, e, f] maps a source point
(x, y) to the destination point

	X = a*x + b*y + c
	Y = d*x + e*y + f

The output canvas is sized to the axis-aligned bounding box of the
transformed source and translated so its top-left corner is the origin.
Exposed areas are filled with opts.Background. The matrix must be invertible
(non-zero determinant of its 2x2 linear part).

methods.name: Bandbool
methods.doc:
Bandbool reduces the three colour channels of each pixel into a single value
by folding them together with a bitwise operator, producing a grayscale
(single-band) image with the result written to R, G and B. Alpha is set fully
opaque.

methods.name: Blur
methods.doc:
Blur applies a Gaussian blur with the given standard deviation (sigma) in
pixels. A sigma <= 0 leaves the image unchanged. The blur is separable and
implemented as two 1-D passes for efficiency; alpha is blurred alongside RGB.

methods.name: Boolean
methods.doc:
Boolean applies a per-channel bitwise operation between the current image and
other, pixel-for-pixel over the overlapping region. The two images must share
dimensions. Alpha is combined with the same operator.

methods.name: Brightness
methods.doc:
Brightness scales pixel intensity. A factor of 1 is a no-op, 0 produces
black, and values above 1 brighten. Negative factors are clamped to 0.

methods.name: CLAHE
methods.doc:
CLAHE applies Contrast Limited Adaptive Histogram Equalisation to the image
luminance and rescales the RGB channels to match, enhancing local contrast
without amplifying noise as strongly as global equalisation. Tile mappings
are bilinearly interpolated to avoid block artefacts. Alpha is preserved.

methods.name: ChannelStats
methods.doc:
ChannelStats computes per-band statistics for the current image and returns a
slice of four ChannelStat values in red, green, blue, alpha order. It mirrors
the richer per-channel information reported by the Node sharp library's
stats() call. An empty image yields zero-valued statistics.

methods.name: Clone
methods.doc:
Clone returns an independent deep copy of the pipeline, including any pending
error state. Mutating the clone never affects the original.

methods.name: Composite
methods.doc:
Composite alpha-blends overlay onto the current image ("source-over"). The
base dimensions are preserved; parts of the overlay outside the base are
clipped.

methods.name: Contrast
methods.doc:
Contrast adjusts contrast about the midpoint (128). A factor of 1 is a no-op,
values above 1 increase contrast and values in [0,1) reduce it.

methods.name: Convolve
methods.doc:
Convolve applies an arbitrary convolution kernel to the RGB channels. Edges
are handled by clamping sample coordinates to the image bounds. The alpha
channel is preserved unchanged.

methods.name: Crop
methods.doc:
Crop extracts the rectangular region r from the current image. It is an alias
for Extract. The region must lie fully within the image bounds.

methods.name: Dilate
methods.doc:
Dilate applies grayscale morphological dilation with a square structuring
element of the given radius (each channel replaced by its neighbourhood
maximum). A radius < 1 is a no-op.

methods.name: DominantColor
methods.doc:
DominantColor returns the most prominent opaque colour in the image. Pixels
are grouped into a coarse 16x16x16 RGB histogram, the most populated bin is
selected, and the mean colour of the pixels falling in that bin is returned
with full opacity. Fully transparent pixels are ignored. It mirrors the
dominant colour reported by the Node sharp library's stats() call.

methods.name: DominantColour
methods.doc: DominantColour is a British-spelling alias for DominantColor.

methods.name: EnsureAlpha
methods.doc:
EnsureAlpha guarantees the image carries an alpha channel. Because the
internal buffer is always RGBA, this is a no-op when any pixel is already
non-opaque; when the image is fully opaque (effectively no alpha information)
every pixel's alpha is set to round(alpha*255), where alpha is in [0,1].

methods.name: Entropy
methods.doc:
Entropy returns the Shannon entropy (in bits) of the image's luminance
histogram. It ranges from 0 for a single-valued image up to 8 for a perfectly
uniform distribution of all 256 luma levels, and is a useful measure of how
much detail an image carries.

methods.name: Erode
methods.doc:
Erode applies grayscale morphological erosion with a square structuring
element of the given radius (each channel replaced by its neighbourhood
minimum). A radius < 1 is a no-op.

methods.name: Err
methods.doc: Err returns the first error that occurred in the pipeline, if any.

methods.name: Extend
methods.signature: func (p *Pipeline) Extend(top, right, bottom, left int, fill RGBA) *Pipeline
methods.doc:
Extend pads the image by the given number of pixels on each side, filling the
new border with fill. Negative values are treated as zero.

methods.name: ExtendWith
methods.doc:
ExtendWith pads the image on each side using the chosen ExtendMode. Unlike
Extend, which always fills with a solid colour, ExtendWith can replicate,
tile or mirror the source pixels into the new border, matching the extendWith
behaviour of the Node sharp library.

methods.name: Extract
methods.doc: Extract extracts the rectangular region r from the current image.

methods.name: ExtractChannel
methods.doc:
ExtractChannel reduces the image to a single band, producing a grayscale
image whose R, G and B are all set to the selected channel's value and whose
alpha is fully opaque.

methods.name: Flatten
methods.doc:
Flatten composites the image over a solid background colour, removing any
transparency. The output is fully opaque.

methods.name: Flip
methods.doc: Flip is an alias for FlipVertical.

methods.name: FlipHorizontal
methods.doc: FlipHorizontal mirrors the image left-to-right.

methods.name: FlipVertical
methods.doc: FlipVertical mirrors the image top-to-bottom.

methods.name: Flop
methods.doc: Flop is an alias for FlipHorizontal.

methods.name: Gamma
Grayscale
methods.doc: Grayscale converts the image to grayscale using Rec. 601 luma weights.

methods.name: Histogram
methods.doc:
Histogram computes per-channel and luminance histograms for the current
image. The counts across each band sum to the pixel count.

methods.name: Invert
methods.doc: Invert is an alias for Negate.

methods.name: IsOpaque
methods.doc:
IsOpaque reports whether every pixel in the image is fully opaque (alpha 255).
It corresponds to the isOpaque flag exposed by the Node sharp library.

methods.name: Linear
methods.doc:
Linear applies an independent per-channel linear transform out = a*in + b to
the RGB channels, with values in [0,255]. Each of a and b may have length 1
(applied to all three channels) or length 3 (R, G, B). Alpha is preserved.

methods.name: MeanColor
methods.doc:
MeanColor returns the arithmetic mean colour of the image across every pixel,
including the alpha channel, rounded to 8-bit components.

methods.name: MeanColour
methods.doc: MeanColour is a British-spelling alias for MeanColor.

methods.name: Median
methods.doc:
Median replaces each pixel with the per-channel median of its
(2*radius+1)x(2*radius+1) neighbourhood, an edge-preserving noise filter.
Edges are handled by clamping sample coordinates. A radius < 1 is a no-op.

methods.name: Metadata
methods.doc:
Metadata returns the dimensions, source format and colour properties of the
current image.

methods.name: Modulate
methods.doc:
Modulate adjusts brightness, saturation, hue and lightness together by
converting each pixel to the cylindrical CIELAB colour space (LCh),
transforming, and converting back. This mirrors the upstream Node.js sharp
library, which performs modulate in LCh: the lightness L* is scaled by
Brightness and offset by Lightness, the chroma C* is scaled by Saturation, and
the hue angle is rotated by Hue degrees. It is a convenient single call for
common tone tweaks. Alpha is preserved.

Because the conversion runs through D65 CIELAB, results match upstream sharp's
documented known-answer vectors to within a rounding unit or two (the upstream
C++/libvips pipeline uses 32-bit float intermediates where this uses float64).

methods.name: Morphology
methods.doc:
Morphology applies the morphological operation op with a square structuring
element of the given radius. Open and Close are compositions of Erode and
Dilate. A radius < 1 is a no-op. RGB and alpha are all processed.

methods.name: Negate
methods.doc: Negate inverts the RGB channels (photographic negative). Alpha is preserved.

methods.name: Normalise
methods.doc:
Normalise stretches image contrast so that the chosen lower/upper luminance
percentiles span the full [0,255] range. The same linear mapping is applied
to all three colour channels, preserving hue. Alpha is untouched. If the
image has no luminance spread the image is left unchanged.

methods.name: Normalize
methods.doc: Normalize is a US-spelling alias for Normalise.

methods.name: Recomb
methods.doc:
Recomb multiplies each pixel's channel vector by a colour matrix. A 3x3
matrix recombines RGB (alpha preserved); a 4x4 matrix recombines RGBA. The
matrix is given row-major as a slice of rows. Results are clamped to [0,255].

methods.name: RemoveAlpha
methods.doc:
RemoveAlpha discards transparency by setting every pixel's alpha to fully
opaque. RGB values are left untouched (they are not pre-multiplied).

methods.name: Resize
methods.doc: Resize scales the image according to opts.

methods.name: ResizeHeight
methods.doc:
ResizeHeight scales the image to the given height in pixels, deriving the
width from the source aspect ratio with bilinear sampling. It is a shorthand
for a Resize with only a height supplied.

methods.name: ResizeTo
methods.doc: ResizeTo is a convenience wrapper for a bilinear FitExact resize.

methods.name: ResizeWidth
methods.doc:
ResizeWidth scales the image to the given width in pixels, deriving the height
from the source aspect ratio with bilinear sampling. It is a shorthand for a
Resize with only a width supplied.

methods.name: Rotate
methods.doc:
Rotate rotates the image by an arbitrary angle in degrees (clockwise) about
its centre using bilinear sampling. The output canvas is enlarged to hold the
whole rotated image and the exposed corners are filled with fill. Multiples
of 90 degrees are dispatched to the exact rotations.

methods.name: Rotate180
methods.doc: Rotate180 rotates the image 180 degrees.

methods.name: Rotate270
methods.doc: Rotate270 rotates the image 270 degrees clockwise (90 counter-clockwise).

methods.name: Rotate90
methods.doc: Rotate90 rotates the image 90 degrees clockwise.

methods.name: Saturation
methods.doc:
Saturation scales colour saturation about the pixel luma. A factor of 0
yields grayscale, 1 is a no-op and values above 1 boost saturation.

methods.name: Sepia
methods.doc:
Sepia applies the classic sepia-tone colour matrix to the image, warming it
into shades of brown. It is a convenience wrapper around the same transform
available through Recomb. Alpha is preserved.

methods.name: Sharpen
methods.doc:
Sharpen applies a standard 3x3 sharpening kernel. Amount scales the
sharpening strength; a value <= 0 leaves the image unchanged and 1 is the
canonical strength.

methods.name: Sharpness
methods.doc:
Sharpness returns a scalar estimate of image sharpness computed as the mean
absolute discrete Laplacian of luminance over the interior pixels. Flat
images score 0; higher values indicate stronger local contrast (edges and
fine detail). Images smaller than 3x3 return 0.

methods.name: Stats
methods.doc: Stats computes the mean of each channel across all pixels.

methods.name: Threshold
methods.doc:
Threshold converts the image to black and white: pixels whose luma is greater
than or equal to level (0-255) become white, the rest black. Alpha is
preserved.

methods.name: Tint
methods.doc:
Tint multiplies each channel by the tint colour (normalised to [0,1]),
producing a colour cast. A tint of White leaves the image unchanged.

methods.name: ToBMP
methods.doc:
ToBMP encodes the current image as an uncompressed BMP. Images with any
transparency are written as 32-bit BGRA; otherwise 24-bit BGR is used.

methods.name: ToColorspace
methods.doc: ToColorspace is a US-spelling alias for ToColourspace.

methods.name: ToColourspace
methods.doc:
ToColourspace converts the working image into the requested colour space.
Recognised values are "b-w", "bw", "grey", "gray", "greyscale" and
"grayscale" (all of which convert to greyscale) and "srgb", "rgb" and "rgb8"
(which leave the sRGB image unchanged). Any other value sets an error on the
pipeline. It corresponds to the toColourspace method of the Node sharp
library.

methods.name: ToFile
methods.signature: func (p *Pipeline) ToFile(path string, format Format, quality int) error
methods.doc:
ToFile encodes the image and writes it to path. The format is chosen from the
requested Format; FormatUnknown falls back to the pipeline's source format,
then to PNG. For JPEG, quality 0 selects the default.

methods.name: ToFormat
methods.signature: func (p *Pipeline) ToFormat(format Format, quality int) ([]byte, error)
methods.doc:
ToFormat encodes the current image to the requested format and returns the
bytes, dispatching to the matching ToPNG/ToJPEG/ToGIF/ToBMP/ToTIFF/ToRaw
method. For JPEG, quality selects the compression level (0 uses the default);
it is ignored for the other formats. FormatUnknown falls back to the
pipeline's source format, then to PNG.

methods.name: ToGIF
methods.doc:
ToGIF encodes the current image as a single-frame GIF. Colours are quantised
to a 256-entry palette by the standard library encoder.

methods.name: ToImage
methods.doc: ToImage returns the resulting image, or the pipeline's error.

methods.name: ToJPEG
methods.doc:
ToJPEG encodes the current image as JPEG at the given quality (1-100). A
quality of 0 selects the default (75).

methods.name: ToJPEGWithOptions
methods.doc:
ToJPEGWithOptions encodes the current image as JPEG using the supplied
options. JPEG has no alpha channel, so the image is flattened onto black.

methods.name: ToPNG
methods.doc: ToPNG encodes the current image as PNG and returns the bytes.

methods.name: ToPNGWithOptions
methods.doc: ToPNGWithOptions encodes the current image as PNG using the supplied options.

methods.name: ToRaw
methods.doc:
ToRaw returns the raw, non-premultiplied 8-bit RGBA pixel bytes of the current
image in row-major order (4 bytes per pixel). The returned slice is a copy.

methods.name: ToTIFF
methods.doc:
ToTIFF encodes the current image as a baseline, uncompressed 8-bit RGBA TIFF
(little-endian).

methods.name: Trim
methods.doc:
Trim auto-crops uniform borders. The reference border colour is taken from
the top-left pixel; any pixel whose per-channel difference from it exceeds
threshold (0-255, compared on the maximum absolute channel delta including
alpha) is treated as content. The image is cropped to the bounding box of the
content. A threshold < 0 is treated as 0. If the whole image matches the
border colour the image is left unchanged.

methods.name: Unflatten
methods.doc:
Unflatten makes fully white pixels transparent by setting the alpha of every
pixel whose red, green and blue channels are all 255 to zero. It is the
inverse-in-spirit of Flatten and mirrors the unflatten operation of the Node
sharp library, useful for turning a white background into transparency.

methods.name: Unsharp
methods.doc:
Unsharp sharpens the image using an unsharp mask: it subtracts a Gaussian-
blurred copy to isolate detail, then adds a scaled multiple of that detail
back. This is the high-quality, sigma-based counterpart to the fixed 3x3
Sharpen. A Sigma <= 0 is a no-op. Alpha is preserved.

methods.name: WithDensity
methods.doc:
WithDensity records a pixel density in dots per inch on the pipeline, which is
reported back through Metadata. Non-positive values are ignored. It mirrors
setting density via the Node sharp library's withMetadata call and does not
alter any pixels.

types.name: RGBA
types.doc:
RGBA is a convenience colour type with 8-bit non-premultiplied channels. It
is used for fills, tints and background colours.

methods.name: NRGBA
methods.doc: NRGBA converts to the standard library's color.NRGBA.

types.name: Rectangle
types.doc: Rectangle describes an axis-aligned region for crop/extract operations.

types.name: ResizeOptions
types.doc:
ResizeOptions configures a Resize operation. A zero Width or Height means
"derive from the other dimension while preserving aspect ratio". At least one
of Width or Height must be non-zero.

types.name: Stats
types.doc:
Stats holds simple per-channel statistics for an image, with each mean in the
range [0,255].

types.name: UnsharpOptions
types.doc: UnsharpOptions configures an Unsharp (unsharp-mask) sharpen.

funcs.name: DeltaE76
funcs.signature: func DeltaE76(l1, a1, b1, l2, a2, b2 float64) float64

funcs.name: HSVToRGB
funcs.signature: func HSVToRGB(h, s, v float64) (r, g, b uint8)
funcs.doc:
HSVToRGB converts a Hue/Saturation/Value colour to an 8-bit sRGB triple. Hue
is taken in degrees (values outside [0,360) are wrapped) and saturation and
value are clamped to [0,1]. It is the inverse of RGBToHSV.

funcs.name: LabToRGB
funcs.signature: func LabToRGB(l, a, bb float64) (r, g, b uint8)
funcs.doc:
LabToRGB converts a CIELAB (L*a*b*) colour under the D65 white point to an
8-bit sRGB triple, clamping to the display gamut. It is the inverse of
RGBToLab.

funcs.name: Luma
funcs.signature: func Luma(r, g, b uint8) uint8
funcs.doc:
Luma returns the rounded Rec. 601 luminance of an 8-bit sRGB triple, using
the weights 0.299R + 0.587G + 0.114B. It is the same luma the library uses
internally for Grayscale and Threshold, exposed for callers that need it.

funcs.name: RGBToHSV
funcs.signature: func RGBToHSV(r, g, b uint8) (h, s, v float64)
funcs.doc:
RGBToHSV converts an 8-bit sRGB triple to Hue/Saturation/Value. Hue is
returned in degrees in the range [0,360), while saturation and value are in
[0,1]. It is the inverse of HSVToRGB. Grey inputs return a hue of 0.

funcs.name: RGBToLab
funcs.signature: func RGBToLab(r, g, b uint8) (l, a, bb float64)
funcs.doc:
RGBToLab converts an 8-bit sRGB triple to CIELAB (L*a*b*) under the D65 white
point. L is in [0,100]; a and b are unbounded but typically within roughly
[-128,127]. Pure white maps to L=100, a=0, b=0 and pure black to L=0. It is
the inverse of LabToRGB.

funcs.name: RGBToXYZ
funcs.signature: func RGBToXYZ(r, g, b uint8) (x, y, z float64)
funcs.doc:
RGBToXYZ converts an 8-bit sRGB triple to CIE 1931 XYZ under the D65 white
point, using the standard sRGB gamma expansion. Y is normalised so that pure
white (255,255,255) maps to approximately (0.9505, 1.0000, 1.0890). It is the
inverse of XYZToRGB.

funcs.name: XYZToRGB
funcs.signature: func XYZToRGB(x, y, z float64) (r, g, b uint8)
funcs.doc:
XYZToRGB converts a CIE 1931 XYZ colour (D65 white point) to an 8-bit sRGB
triple, applying the sRGB gamma and clamping to the display gamut. It is the
inverse of RGBToXYZ.

examples.doc:
Example builds a small gradient image, resizes it, and prints the resulting
bounds and format.
